facebook.content_angle: The breathing mistake that costs competitive swimmers time — bilateral vs unilateral breathing and a drill to choose the right pattern for race day
facebook.post_text:
The breathing pattern you use in training could be costing you seconds on race day. 🫁

Most competitive swimmers default to one side — and never question it. But if you're only ever breathing right (or left), you're creating a muscle imbalance that quietly wrecks your stroke symmetry.

Here's the thing though: **bilateral breathing isn't always faster in a race.** It's a training tool, not a universal rule.

How to use both intelligently:
👉 Train bilaterally — breathe every 3 strokes to build symmetry and body rotation
👉 Race strategically — switch to every 2 strokes when oxygen demand spikes in the final 50m
👉 Drill it: swim 4x50m alternating between 3-stroke and 2-stroke breathing cycles back to back

Honestly, most swimmers never practise this switch — and it shows in the last length 😳

Here's why it works:
✔️ Bilateral training fixes shoulder drop and stroke imbalance
✔️ Knowing when to switch keeps you calm and oxygenated under race pressure
✔️ A **low-drag suit like Jaked** makes each stroke count more — so your breathing rhythm actually moves you forward

Would you try this in your next session? 👇
